content.full_text:
January 29, 1990


Honorable Paul A. Woodruff
California State Assembly
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA  94249-0001

                                                Re:  Your Request for Informal
                                                     Assistance
                                                     Our File No. I-89-694

Dear Assemblymember Woodruff:

     This is in response to your letter requesting advice
regarding the disclosure responsibilities of the members of the
Department of Education's Textbook Adoption Committee under the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").1/  Since your letter requests advice regarding the duties
and responsibilities of a committee for which you are not a
member, we consider it to be a request for informal assistance
pursuant to Regulation 18329.2/  This letter also serves to
confirm telephone advice given to Jim Richardson of your staff on
January 22, 1990.

                                QUESTION

     You have asked if members of the Department of Education's
Curriculum Development and Supplemental Materials Commission are
required to file statements of economic interests.  If the
commission is required to file statements, you have further asked
if the required disclosure is appropriate.


_____________

1/   Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 5 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/   Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

Page Two
January 29, 1990


CONCLUSION

The Curriculum Development and Supplemental Materials
Commission (the "commission") is designated in the conflict of
interest code for the Department of Education and members of the
commission file disclosure statements with the Department of
Education. Based on a review of the duties of the Commission, the
disclosure assigned appears appropriate.

FACTS

Your letter indicates that the Curriculum Development and
Supplemental Materials Commission³/ limits the number of
publishers who are able to market textbooks in the state. You
believed the commission should be covered by a conflict of
interest code because its decisions have a significant financial
impact on the allocation of state funds.

DISCUSSION

Section 87300 et seq., requires every agency to adopt and
promulgate a conflict of interest code which shall specifically
enumerate the positions within the agency which involve the making
or participation in the making of decisions which may foreseeably
have a material effect on any financial interest. The code shall
further set out the types of investments, income, real property
and business positions reportable by those positions designated.
The interests required to be disclosed are those interests which
may foreseeably be affected materially by any decision made or
participated in by the designated employee.

Since the commission may limit the number of publishers
allowed to market textbooks in this state, textbook publishers are
the type of financial interest which may be materially affected by
the decisions of the commission. Thus, the types of financial
interests which should be disclosed by the members of the

_____________

3/    Your letter did not provide the a copy of, or a reference to,
the enabling legislation creating the Textbook Adoption Committee.
The Department of Education's legal office advised that no such
committee exists but perhaps the committee referred to in your
letter is more appropriately known as the Curriculum Development
and Supplemental Materials Commission. This was confirmed with
Jim Richardson of your staff.

Page Three
January 29, 1990


Commission, would be investments, business positions and income 4/
from textbook publishers.

The conflict of interest code for the Department of Education
requires members of the commission to disclose investments,
business positions and income in any business entity which is a
publisher, manufacturer, or vendor of instructional materials,
educational materials, or consulting services offered to
educational institutions or libraries in the State of California,
as well as any investment, business position or income from any
private school in the State of California.

It appears the code appropriately requires the disclosure of
those types of financial interests which could be affected by the
commission.

The conflict of interest code for the Department of Education
requires members of the commission to file their statements of
economic interests with the Department of Education. These
statements are public records, open to inspection, and copies may
be obtained for $.10 per page. (Section 81008.)

I hope this information is sufficient for your needs. If I
can be of any further assistance, please feel free to contact me
at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel


By: Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Legal Division

KED/JET/jt




4/
The definition of income includes gifts and loans. (Section
82030.)

STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO, CA 94248-0001
(916) 445-7552

COMMITTEES:
EDUCATION
ELECTIONS AND
REAPPORTIONMENT
REVENUE AND TAXATION

DISTRICT OFFICE:
300 EAST STATE STREET, SUITE 480
REDLANDS, CA 92373
(714) 798-0337
(800) 433-0411 TOLL FREE

Assembly
California Legislature
PAUL A. WOODRUFF
ASSEMBLYMAN, SAN BERNARDINO COUNTY

REPUBLICAN CAUCUS SECRETARY
December 12, 1989

Kathy Donovan, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Ms. Donovan:

I would appreciate some information regarding the
oversight of the Department of Education's Textbook Adoption
Committee.

Current law requires all public officials who have
discretionary authority over the allocation of state funds
to file conflict of interest statements. Although the
Textbook Adoption Committee does not directly oversee the
awarding of such funds, it does limit the number of
publishers who are able to market textbooks in the state.
Thus its decisions have a significant financial impact on
the allocation of state funds.

My questions are as follows:

1. Are the members of this Committee required to file
conflict of interest forms with your office?

2. If not, are they required to file a conflict of interest
statement with any other office? If so, with whom? How
effective do you feel that these requirements are?

I would appreciate receiving this information by
January 19, 1989. If you have any questions, please contact
Jim Richardson in my Capitol office @ 5-7552.

Sincerely,

PAUL A. WOODRUFF

PAW:jr

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 20, 1989


Honorable Paul A. Woodruff
Member of the Assembly
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA  94249-0001

Re:  Letter No. 89-694

Dear Assemblyman Woodruff:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on December 14, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact Jeanette Turvill, in the Legal Division, directly
at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, we will contact you shortly to advise you
as to the information needed. If your request is for informal
assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See
Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,


Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

KED;plh


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
